title: How File-Grounded AI Reduces Hallucinations in Analytics
summary: Generic AI invents numbers; file-grounded AI answers only from columns you uploaded. Here is why grounding matters for business analytics.

content.summary: Generic AI invents numbers; file-grounded AI answers only from columns you uploaded. Here is why grounding matters for business analytics.

body.text: When an AI chatbot quotes revenue that never appeared in your export, the problem is not the model—it is the missing link to your file. File-grounded systems bind questions to column names, row filters, and aggregates computed on the upload in front of you. The model cannot silently import industry benchmarks or training-set trivia when the scope is your CSV. Teams that preview schema before asking questions catch mismatches early: wrong date column, amounts stored as text, duplicate keys that inflate totals.

body.alt: Analytics dashboard with charts on a laptop screen
body.caption: Analytics dashboard with charts on a laptop screen. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Grounding means every answer references your dataset

File-grounded systems bind questions to column names, row filters, and aggregates computed on the upload in front of you. The model cannot silently import industry benchmarks or training-set trivia when the scope is your CSV.
Teams that preview schema before asking questions catch mismatches early: wrong date column, amounts stored as text, duplicate keys that inflate totals.

items: Questions map to explicit fields visible in preview.
Filters applied before AI match filters on your charts.
Responses cite aggregates you can recompute manually.
No answer draws on web knowledge unrelated to the upload.

body.text: Pair every AI total with a bar chart or filtered row count built from the same file. Discrepancies usually trace to ambiguous column labels or rows your filter excluded.
Document verified prompts as team templates so newcomers ask grounded questions instead of free-form guesses.
Datilio's Talk to Data runs on the file you inspected—upload, preview, filter, then ask—so hallucination risk drops because the scope is visible.
